Advocates of the initiative and
referendum laws say this is a view
of the ordinance which they had
never taken but declare that they
cannot give up principle which
means so much to all the people
because 1t may not meet the ap-
prevail of those interests which at
times seek special favors. The
aim claimed by the referendum
people is to make this a govern
ment ruled by the majority and
not by the few.


1t is argued by the referendum
supporters that invariably the in
terests seeking a monopoly such
as the Washington Water Power
company and concerns of like
character occupy the same polti-
cal bed as Dutch" Jake, Harry
Bare and Jack Wilmot. Both
should, so the advocates of the ref
erendum argue, be made amend
able to the law.
